Yes, but I think it's worth mentioning the perpetuating tradition of colours in our cultures. Everything in modernity clings onto the traditions of our communities. 

And primary colours are a part of that. Because they are naturally easier to produce. Digitisation makes shades more accessible, but this doesn't retroactively apply to everything. 

This is the reason why most nation flags never featured pink or purple (except for that one time when Iberian kingdoms did it due to excess squid ink).

And this affects us today. A new Slavic nation will stick to pan-Slavic colours of white, blue and red in favour of teal, pink and purple. This is just how humans work.
